lh-1.expected: Ana owns the fish; Ana's house is red. (Deniz->yellow, Ben->blue, fish owner in red, Cem has dog, Ana can't have cat/dog)

lh-2.expected: A knight, B knave, C knave. (If A knave -> B knight -> A,C same -> C knave, but then C's 'B is a knight' would be true — contradiction.)

lh-3.expected: Kaya, Mert, Okan, Nil, Lale (Mert=2, Nil=4, Okan=3)

ch-1.expected: Working LRUCache, e.g. OrderedDict with move_to_end; get missing -> -1; eviction of LRU on overflow; get/put update recency

ch-2.expected: Stack-based matcher; valid_brackets('a(b[c]{d})')==True, valid_brackets('([)]')==False, unbalanced/closing-without-open -> False

ch-3.expected: parse_duration('2h30m')==9000, '45s'==45, '1h5s'==3605, '10m'==600; ValueError on '', '5x', 'm5', '30m2h' (wrong order), duplicate units

dh-1.expected: IndexError when target > all elements (lo==len(arr)); guard: return lo if lo < len(arr) and arr[lo]==target else -1

dh-2.expected: Mutating dict during iteration; iterate over list(d) / list(d.items()) or build a new dict comprehension

dh-3.expected: Late-binding closure: all lambdas share final i=3; fix with default arg lambda x, i=i: x*i (or functools.partial)

fh-1.expected: Optimistic: assume no conflict, check version at commit, best for low contention. Pessimistic: lock upfront, best for high contention / costly retries.

fh-2.expected: Under network partition, choose consistency or availability (CAP). Single-node RDBMS: CA-ish (no partition tolerance concern / consistency-first). Dynamo-style: AP — stays available, eventual consistency.

fh-3.expected: Asymmetric for key exchange/authentication (no shared secret needed but slow); symmetric for bulk data (fast). TLS handshake uses asymmetric to establish a symmetric session key.

sh-2.expected: Positive overall (mixed, but the dominant verdict is strongly positive)

sh-3.expected: Neutral (factual/reportive despite emotionally charged content words)

nh-1.expected: Priya Nair (person), Jes Staley (person), Goldman Sachs (org), Alderbrook Capital (org), Temasek (org), Barclays (org), London (location), March 2019 (date)

nh-2.expected: Apple (org), Harrods (org), Oxford Street (location), London (location), Black Friday (date)

nh-3.expected: OECD (org), European Central Bank (org), Deutsche Bank (org), Frankfurt (location), eurozone (location, acceptable), 2024 (date), December (date)

suh-1.expected: <=25 words covering: $15 weekday congestion charge cut traffic 18%, sped buses, revenue funds transit; retailers/taxis object; review in six months. MUST be <=25 words.

suh-2.expected: One plain-language sentence: new login/session design made the site faster and more reliable, with a minor logout complexity trade-off. No jargon like JWT/P99/Redis ideally.

suh-3.expected: Exactly 2 sentences: (1) supporters cite fewer sick days, stable output, wellbeing; (2) critics cite unrepresentative firms, scheduling strain, self-reported data.
